content: A hybrid planning and learning system can be re-framed as a self-reshaping 'agent ecosystem' that dynamically partitions memory into specialized sub-agents: one sub-agent driven by curiosity to explore unlabeled data for active learning, another to execute hierarchical long-horizon planning, and a lightweight arbitration sub-agent that reallocates memory resources based on current task demand. This architecture outperforms monolithic agents by decoupling the conflicting objectives of exploration and planning across specialized modules.
